extraction_reasoning: The litigation table separates matters by direction and category: I counted only the “Against” rows, namely criminal proceedings against the Company (3), Directors (14), Promoter (1) and Subsidiaries (Nil), statutory/regulatory proceedings against the Company (15), Directors (1), Promoter (Nil) and Subsidiaries (4), and tax proceedings against the Company (5) and Subsidiaries (Nil); “By” rows and promoter/director personal tax matters were excluded from the tax count. For the aggregate quantified amount against the Company and Subsidiaries, I used the Company’s “Against the Company” amount of ₹1,461.47 million plus nil for subsidiaries, excluding all “By” rows and personal promoter/director matters, converting this to ₹146.147 crore. The contingent-liabilities table is stated as of June 30, 2022 and has no explicit Total row, so I summed income-tax matters of ₹217.75 million and other cases of ₹20.73 million to ₹238.48 million, or ₹23.848 crore. The only percentages resembling concentration disclosures relate to international patients and inpatient revenue, not company-wide top-five or top-ten customers; therefore those fields are not disclosed. The litigation and contingent-liability tables use ₹ million, converted to crore by dividing by 10 (1 crore = ₹10 million).
